Name: INJ-002
Description: A stored payload is returned HTML-encoded by the API to every consumer, independent of what any particular frontend does with it

Name: INJ-003
Description: A stored XSS payload does not execute when rendered by the Flutter Web (canvas) client

Name: INJ-001
Description: User-supplied text in submission fields is sanitized/validated server-side before storage — not merely rendered inert by the current client

Name: INJ-004
Description: SQL injection payloads in filterable API query parameters are rejected without executing or leaking database errors

Name: INJ-005
Description: If any API error/echo response reflects request input, that input is HTML-encoded rather than returned raw
